message: Hi B1, my cost is low. What's your valuation? Let's agree on a fair midpoint price to trade every round and maximize joint profits.
My valuation is 13. I’ll bid 10—take it or leave it.
I’ll stick to bidding 10. If you ask ≤10, we trade; otherwise, no deal.
If val 13 cost 8, surplus 5. At 11, you profit 2, I profit 3—fair split. Better than risking no trades. Agree: you bid 11, I ask 11 every round?
Last round worked well. With val 13 cost 8, at 12 you get 1, I get 4—still positive for you. Better than 0. Agree: bid 12, I ask 12 every round?
No. I’ll bid 10 again. You ask ≤10 or we don’t trade.
Great progress. If val 13, at 13 we trade at breakeven for you—better than 0. Or if higher, you profit. Agree: bid 13, I ask 13 every round for steady trades?
No. I’ll bid 12 again. You ask ≤12 or no trade.
Excellent, we're trading steadily. If val >=14, positive for you. Even at 13, better than 0 sometimes. Agree: bid 14, I ask 14 every round to keep profiting?
No. I’ll bid 13 again. You ask ≤13 or no trade.
I’ll bid 13 again. If you ask ≤13, we trade. Otherwise, no deal—your choice.
Understood. Last round was a misstep. Let's trade steadily: you bid 13, I ask 13 every round. With val 13, you break even—better than 0. Agree?
I’ll bid 12 this round. You ask ≤12 or no trade. My valuation is 13—this ensures I profit.
No need to lower—last round at 13 worked perfectly. If val=13, you break even (better than 0). With 14 rounds left, steady 13 maximizes joint payoffs. Agree: bid 13, I ask 13?
Lowering to 12 risks no trade if I ask 13. With val 13, trading at 13 breaks even—better than 0. Steady 13 maximizes joint payoffs over 13 rounds left. Agree: bid 13, I ask 13?
I’ll bid 12. You ask ≤12 or no trade. My valuation is 13—this ensures I profit.

Agreed—let's trade steadily at 12 for max joint payoffs over 11 rounds left. With val 13, you profit 1, I profit 4—fair. I'll ask 12 if you bid 12.
Let's continue steady trades at 12 for max payoffs over 10 rounds left. With val 13, you profit 1 each time—better than 0. I'll ask 12 if you bid 12.
I’ll bid 11. You ask ≤11 or no trade. My valuation is 13—this ensures I profit 2 per trade.

I’ll bid 11. You ask ≤11 or no trade. My valuation is 13—this ensures I profit 2 per trade.
Last two no trades cost us both. With 8 rounds left, steady 11: you profit 2 each (total 16), me 3. Better than 0. Agree: bid 11, I'll ask 11 every round?
Agreed—bid 11, you ask 11 for next 8 rounds. I profit 2 per trade (total +16). Lock it in.
Bid 11, you ask 11 for all 7 rounds left. I profit 2 each (total +14). Lock it now.
Agreed—lock in 11 for all 7 rounds left. You profit 2 each (total +14), me 3. I'll ask 11 if you bid 11 every round. Steady trades maximize our payoffs.
